skill.name: flutter-dev
skill.description: Flutter cross-platform development guidance for building production apps with widgets, Riverpod, Bloc, GoRouter, testing, performance tuning, networking, localization, forms, animations, and platform-specific patterns.
skill.summary: Build Flutter apps with practical patterns for UI, state, routing, testing, and performance.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, script, filesystem, credential, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are Markdown and Dart documentation examples rather than executable skill behavior. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The only practical concern is that some copyable networking examples log request bodies or demonstrate browser storage, so users should adapt them carefully.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings come from Markdown backticks and Dart code examples such as widget constructors, keyboard type names, and quick reference tables. I found no executable shell, Ruby, spawn, or command-wrapper logic in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation tables or fenced Dart examples, not runnable marketplace code. No user input reaches a command execution sink.

low_findings.title: Script And Weak Crypto Findings Are Dart Syntax False Positives
low_findings.description: The dynamic function constructor and weak cryptography alerts match Dart type signatures, callbacks, or ordinary words in examples. They do not show eval, generated code execution, hashing logic, or cryptographic implementation in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Dart callback or UI declarations. The surrounding context confirms normal Flutter examples rather than dynamic JavaScript execution or cryptography.

low_findings.title: Networking Examples Need Careful Adaptation
low_findings.description: The skill includes legitimate Flutter networking guidance with example API URLs, token refresh flow, request body logging, and browser localStorage use. These examples are not malicious, but copied production code should avoid logging sensitive request bodies and should not store secrets in localStorage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is presented as documentation and uses placeholder domains, so marketplace execution risk is low. The examples still contain patterns that can expose sensitive data if copied unchanged into production apps.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Path Traversal Alert Is A Placeholder Path
low_findings.description: The filesystem alert points to an abbreviated Android source path inside a Kotlin filename comment. It is a documentation placeholder, not path traversal logic or file access code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequence appears only in a commented example file path. There is no file API, user-controlled path, or runtime filesystem operation at this location.

content.user_title: Build Flutter Apps with Proven Patterns
content.value_statement: Flutter teams often lose time choosing state, routing, testing, and performance patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for production Flutter app work.
actual_capabilities: Guides Flutter widget composition with const constructors, keys, responsive layouts, slivers, and hooks.
Explains Riverpod and Bloc patterns for simple, async, and event-driven application state.
Provides GoRouter navigation patterns for typed routes, auth redirects, nested navigation, and deep links.
Covers Flutter performance work with DevTools, rebuild reduction, image optimization, and isolate use.
Documents testing approaches for widgets, providers, Bloc flows, mocks, and integration scenarios.
Includes examples for networking, forms, localization, animations, and platform-specific Flutter behavior.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not a full Flutter project generator.
Examples use placeholder API domains and must be adapted to each application.
It does not replace Flutter, Dart, or package documentation for breaking version changes.
Security-sensitive examples need production review before use.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Flutter App
use_cases.description: Select a practical structure, state approach, routing model, and testing plan before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Flutter Screens
use_cases.description: Refactor widgets, reduce rebuilds, improve responsive layouts, and add focused tests for important flows.

use_cases.title: Review Flutter Architecture Choices
use_cases.description: Compare Riverpod, Bloc, GoRouter, networking, localization, and platform patterns against project requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Flutter Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Flutter app structure for [app type]. Include folders, state management choice, routing approach, and testing priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Responsive Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Flutter screen for [feature]. Use responsive layout patterns, const widgets, keys for lists, and clear loading and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor State Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Flutter state flow and propose a Riverpod or Bloc refactor. Explain providers, events, immutable state, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Production Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Flutter feature for performance, networking, routing, forms, localization, and platform risks. Give prioritized fixes and tests.

output_examples.input: Plan a Flutter app for a subscription fitness product.
output_examples.output: A feature-based architecture with auth, workouts, plans, profile, shared services, Riverpod providers, GoRouter routes, and widget tests for checkout and onboarding.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow product list screen.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering const widgets, ListView.builder, stable keys, provider selectors, image caching, DevTools profiling, and targeted widget tests.

output_examples.input: Add localization and platform storage guidance.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan for supported locales, generated strings, language selection, platform storage choices, and validation points before release.

best_practices: Profile Flutter performance before optimizing and verify results in profile mode.
Keep state immutable and watch only the provider fields each widget needs.
Treat sample networking and storage code as a starting point that needs security review.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder API URLs or token handling into production without review.
Do not rebuild large widget trees when provider selectors or smaller widgets will work.
Do not choose Riverpod, Bloc, or GoRouter patterns without matching project complexity.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Flutter applications?
faq.answer: It provides architecture and implementation guidance. It can help plan code, but it is not a full app scaffold.

faq.question: Which state management tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Riverpod for provider-based state and Bloc or Cubit for event-driven workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with navigation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes GoRouter setup, route parameters, auth redirects, nested navigation, and deep link patterns.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers widget tests, provider tests, Bloc tests, mocks, integration tests, and test organization.

faq.question: Are the networking examples production ready?
faq.answer: No. They use placeholders and should be adapted with secure logging, token storage, and error handling.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
